data.title: Spot silver returns to the $100 mark
data.description: Spot silver rebounded after a decline, regaining the $100 mark, and rising more than $5 from its intraday low. (Jinshi)

data.title: Spot Silver Rebounds, Surpasses $100 Mark
data.description: Spot silver has halted its decline and rebounded, climbing back above the $100 threshold. According to Odaily, the price has increased by more than $5 from its intraday low.

data.title: Canadian Dollar Faces Pressure Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description:
On January 30, analysts from ING, including Francisco Pesole, highlighted potential challenges for the Canadian dollar. According to Jin10, the currency may weaken if the U.S. dollar continues its upward trend, negotiations for the renewal of the USMCA agreement stall, and Canada's unemployment rate rises further. The deteriorating relationship between the U.S. and Canada, along with employment market issues, could increase expectations for further interest rate cuts by the Bank of Canada.

The Bank of Canada is closely monitoring the quarterly business outlook survey. Despite improvements in other economic confidence indicators, this survey consistently shows that trade uncertainty was the main reason for the decline in hiring intentions among businesses in the fourth quarter. ING suggests that the USD/CAD exchange rate could rise to the range of 1.36-1.37.

data.title: Spot gold has returned to the $5,100 mark.
data.description: Spot gold has climbed back above the $5,100/ounce mark, rebounding nearly $160 from its intraday low, narrowing its intraday losses to approximately 5%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Circle Advances Arc Blockchain Development Amid Stablecoin Competition
data.description:
Circle is advancing the development of Arc, its Layer-1 blockchain, with the aim of establishing a global financial operating system. According to NS3.AI, the initiative is marked by significant institutional involvement and aims to achieve near-instant transaction finality. Circle is striving to expand its market share in stablecoin settlements by enhancing the Circle Payments Network and introducing innovative interoperability tools. This move comes as the company faces strong competition from Tether, which currently holds over 60% of the stablecoin market share.

Major financial institutions, including BlackRock and Visa, are increasingly adopting blockchain-based settlement solutions. This trend highlights a potential shift towards the use of stablecoins in regulated treasury operations, signaling growing acceptance and integration of blockchain technology in traditional financial systems.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,863, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on these exchanges could reach $1.399 billion. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $2,593, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions could amount to $625 million.

data.title: The Bhutanese government transferred 100.82 BTC, worth $8.31 million, to QCP Capital.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, the Bhutanese government transferred 100.82 BTC to QCP Capital, worth $8.31 million, and further transfers may continue.

data.title: Crypto Market Mood Weakens With Fear Index At Lowest Level Since December
data.description:

data.title: After making a profit of $2.7 million, pension-usdt.eth shorted 30,000 ETH with 3x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, pension-usdt.eth (0x0ddf...a902) has reopened a 3x leveraged short position on ETH after profiting $2.7 million in a previous transaction. This address currently holds 30,000 ETH short positions, worth $81.8 million, with an entry price of $2,746 and a liquidation price of $3,713.44.

data.title: The market value of international gold and silver has evaporated by a huge amount.
data.description: International spot silver and spot gold prices plummeted during the day, both falling below the $100 and $5,000 marks respectively. Data from Companiesmarketcap shows that the global market capitalization of silver shrank by 16.45% to $5.382 trillion, while the market capitalization of gold evaporated by 6.59% to $34.779 trillion. However, both still rank first and second in global market capitalization, with silver in second place significantly ahead of Nvidia ($4.687 trillion), which ranks third. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitget Wallet launches USD-compliant stablecoin USA₮
data.description: According to official news from Bitget Wallet, the platform has launched USA₮, a USD-compliant stablecoin issued by Anchorage Digital Bank. Users can view its price, track their holdings, and trade it in real time within their wallets. USA₮ is a USD-pegged stablecoin regulated by the US government and launched in partnership with Tether and Anchorage Digital Bank, N.A. It complies with the GENIUS Act and aims to provide businesses, institutions, and individuals with a digital alternative to traditional USD payments, helping them use digital dollars more conveniently and without borders. However, USA₮ is not legal tender in the United States, nor is it issued, guaranteed, approved, or protected by the US government. USA₮ is not insured by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), the Securities Investor Protection Corporation (SIPC), or any other government agency.

data.title: Ethereum Co-Founder Withdraws $17.3 Million for Ecosystem Initiatives
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has withdrawn $17.3 million worth of ether from the Ethereum Foundation. According to NS3.AI, the withdrawal is intended to support initiatives that enhance openness and verifiability within the Ethereum ecosystem. This action aligns with the foundation's broader strategy to optimize spending while concentrating on strategic long-term objectives.

data.title: Understanding Binance's Unique Work Culture and Core Values
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, offering an in-depth look into the unique work culture at Binance, a company known for its high-performance and results-driven environment. The article aims to provide potential employees with a realistic perspective on what it means to work at Binance, emphasizing the company's five core values: protecting users, reducing friction, staying fast and autonomous, taking direct feedback, and collaborating globally without unnecessary meetings.

The article begins by highlighting the freedom that comes with working at Binance, which operates on a global and remote-first basis. Employees are expected to adapt to different time zones, which can be challenging for those who prefer a structured 9-5 schedule. However, this freedom allows employees to manage their schedules and outputs, creating a work rhythm that suits their personal lives. This autonomy comes with accountability, as employees are trusted to own their time, outcomes, and impact. For those who thrive in a flexible environment and can handle the responsibility that comes with it, Binance offers a unique opportunity.

Humility is another core value at Binance, where employees are encouraged to take ownership and make decisions independently. This environment can be intense, as it requires individuals to handle complex tasks, address issues, and remain open to feedback, even when it is direct and unfiltered. The article emphasizes the importance of humility in accepting feedback, learning from mistakes, and improving work quality. This approach fosters a culture of continuous improvement and collaboration, where employees are expected to focus on outcomes rather than personal pride.

Collaboration at Binance is characterized by a flat hierarchy, where teams are empowered to make decisions quickly and efficiently. The company values straightforward communication, shared goals, and a bias toward action. Employees are encouraged to document decisions and move work forward in a way that others can build upon, fostering trust and collaboration even in an asynchronous environment. This approach may feel unfamiliar to those accustomed to strict chains of command, but it allows for rapid progress and innovation.

The 'Hardcore' value at Binance reflects the fast-paced nature of the cryptocurrency industry, where employees are expected to learn quickly, iterate, and adapt to uncertainty. The article describes how employees are encouraged to tackle high-impact problems, deliver initial solutions, and continuously improve them. This mindset requires resilience, curiosity, and a focus on outcomes, as the ultimate goal is to serve the users effectively.

Finally, the article underscores the importance of being user-focused, as Binance's mission is to create products that are genuinely useful and secure for users. Employees are expected to prioritize user satisfaction, ensuring clarity, removing friction, and maintaining trust. This user-centric approach is the driving force behind Binance's operations, and those who share this commitment will find a fulfilling role within the company.

In conclusion, the article makes it clear that Binance is not for everyone. It is a place for individuals who are ready to embrace high standards, take real ownership, and make a tangible impact in the world. Those seeking comfort and predictability may find other environments more suitable, but for builders who thrive on challenge and innovation, Binance offers a unique and rewarding opportunity.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks generally fell in pre-market trading in the US, with Strategy (MSTR) down 9.63%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks generally fell in pre-market trading in the US. Strategy (MSTR) fell 9.63%, MARA Holdings (MARA) fell 4.92%, Sharplink Gaming (SBET) fell 6.02%, BitMine (BMNR) fell 9.89%, and DeFi Development Corp (DFDV) fell 11.77%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.
